title: Add a runtime permission flow
description: Wire a dangerous permission from manifest declaration through request, rationale, grant, and graceful denial.
body: Steps: 1. Declare the permission in AndroidManifest. 2. At point of use, check whether it's already granted. 3. If not, decide whether to show a rationale (shouldRationale), then launch the request via the permissions API. 4. Handle the result: proceed on grant, degrade gracefully on denial, and detect permanent denial (route to settings). 5. Test grant, deny-once, and deny-permanently paths on a real OS version. Rollback point: before shipping a feature gated on a new permission (Play policy review). Validation: the feature works after grant and never crashes on any denial path; calling the protected API assuming it's granted is the SecurityException-causing bug this flow prevents.
